goal: Kill the temporal shimmer on animated metallic panels without losing chrome fidelity.
thoughts: Denoiser shimmer on chrome is a known path-tracing tradeoff -- temporally-unstable caustics on near-zero roughness. Real diagnostic order: roughness floor first (cheapest test), denoiser temporal stability second, samples last. Never outright disable the denoiser -- that trades shimmer for grain. Acknowledge art direction may veto roughness bump; have a Plan B.
code:
mats = list_all_prims(type_filter="Material", path_prefix="/World/Hero")
# Test 1: raise roughness floor 0.05-0.10 on metallic panels
mat = create_material(
    path="/World/Looks/Chrome_RoughFloor",
    shader="MDL",
    mdl_source="<MDL_MODULE>",
    params={"metallic": 1.0, "roughness": "<ROUGHNESS+0.05>"}
)
assign_material(prim_path="/World/Hero/Body", material_path=mat.path)
capture_viewport(path="<TEST_PNG>", width=1920, height=1080)
# Test 2: denoiser temporal / sample tweaks if roughness vetoed
set_render_config(
    samples_per_pixel="<HIGHER_SPP>",
    denoiser="optix",
    denoiser_temporal_stability="<FLOAT>",
    firefly_clamp="<FLOAT>"
)
failure_modes: Disabling the denoiser entirely and handing her grain instead of shimmer
Pushing samples first without testing the cheap roughness fix
Treating path-traced animation shimmer as a tool bug rather than a documented tradeoff
